data.title: Semiconductor industry revenue hits record $216.3bn in 3Q25, up 14.5% QoQ, on track to exceed $800bn in 2025.
data.description: In London, new findings from Omdia reveal that the semiconductor industry achieved remarkable results in 3Q25, with global revenue hitting a record high of $216.3 billion, showing a significant increase of 14.5% compared to the previous quarter. This milestone marks the first time the industry has crossed the $200 billion mark in a single quarter, building on the strong performance seen in 2Q25, which experienced an 8% QoQ growth. With this impressive growth trend, the industry is projected to surpass a total revenue of $800 billion for the entire year of 2025. The unexpected surge in demand in 3Q25 has far exceeded the usual seasonal expectations, highlighting the robustness of the semiconductor market.

data.title: 54% of consumers reduce nonessential spending due to tariffs, affecting budgets and confidence in economy.
data.description: In San Mateo, California on December 11, 2025, reports show that a significant number of Americans are facing financial constraints due to tariffs impacting their budgets and confidence in the economy. Approximately 54% of consumers have either already implemented or are planning to implement cutbacks on nonessential expenses in response to price increases linked to tariffs. This highlights a growing concern among the populace regarding the financial impact of these trade policies on their livelihoods.

data.title: Sanborn Map Co. renews ISO 9001:2015 certification, expands to include Sanborn Geophysics in Ontario. Globally recognized quality management standard.
data.description: The Sanborn Map Company, Inc. (Sanborn Geospatial) based in Colorado Springs, has recently renewed its ISO 9001:2015 certification, a prestigious global standard for quality management systems. This certification renewal highlights Sanborn's dedication to maintaining exceptional quality in their geospatial information solutions. Notably, the scope of certification has been broadened to encompass Sanborn Geophysics in Ontario, Canada, where the company manufactures geophysical sensors. This expansion underscores Sanborn's commitment to excellence not only in their core geospatial products but also in their geophysical sensor manufacturing, further solidifying their position as a leading provider in the industry.

data.title: Dollar Tree's rally continues near 52-week high, but growth ranking drops due to lackluster forecast.
data.description: Shares of Dollar Tree Inc. (NASDAQ: DLTR) are currently near their 52-week highs, but recent fundamental signals suggest caution. The discount retailer's growth score dropped significantly this week, falling from 15.25 to 7.77, ranking it in the bottom 10th percentile for this metric. Despite a rally in stock price following a strong third-quarter earnings report, the decline in growth ranking indicates a disconnect between profitability and revenue expansion potential. Dollar Tree's updated fiscal 2025 outlook contributed to this drop, as management revised full-year sales guidance to approximately $19.35 billion. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: 2025 Mazda CX-90 now available in Bayamón, Puerto Rico at Flagship Mazda. Customers can explore this model for a bold driving experience.
data.description: Residents of Bayamón, Puerto Rico, looking for a standout vehicle can now check out the 2025 Mazda CX-90 at Flagship Mazda. As of December 11, 2025, drivers in Bayamón have the opportunity to experience the bold and versatile features of this model. This vehicle promises a new standard of performance and sophistication for customers in the area.

data.title: Coinbase offers access to Solana DEX, allowing trading of any Solana token without formal listing as CeFi and DeFi merge.
data.description: Coinbase has recently connected to Solana's rapidly expanding DeFi ecosystem, enabling users to trade any Solana token through DEX rails without the need for a formal listing process. This integration aims to provide users with more flexibility and access to the numerous tokens available on Solana's platform. This move reflects the increasing popularity and adoption of Solana in the market.

data.title: Temu collaborates with 1,500+ brands to enhance trademark protection, IP enforcement on and off platform.
data.description: Temu, the global online marketplace based in Boston, has recently announced its collaboration with over 1,500 brands as part of its Brand Guardian Initiative. Launched on December 11, 2025, this initiative aims to enhance intellectual property (IP) protection both on and off the platform. By directly partnering with a significant number of brands, Temu is taking a proactive approach to safeguarding IP rights and fostering a more secure online environment.

data.title: Eli Lilly's obesity drug showed 23.7% weight loss in trial, stock rose.
data.description: Eli Lily's shares surged past a significant threshold following the announcement that their new obesity treatment has shown impressive results in a recent phase three trial, resulting in a weight loss of 23.7%. This data marks a significant achievement for the company in the fight against obesity.

data.title: Garry Ridge showed humility as WD-40 CEO since 1997, took a DISC profile test, per Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: In 1997, Garry Ridge assumed the role of chief executive at WD-40. Following this, he underwent a personality assessment known as the DISC profile. Ridge's leadership style at WD-40 has been shaped by his humility, as outlined in a recent article from Investor's Business Daily.
